# Nepali DatePicker Studio

> The most advanced Bikram Sambat (BS) date picker library for modern web applications.
> Built with pure vanilla JavaScript — zero dependencies, zero frameworks.

## What is this?

Nepali DatePicker Studio is a JavaScript library that renders an interactive calendar widget
for the Nepali Bikram Sambat (BS) calendar system. It handles the full BS date lifecycle:
display, selection, bidirectional conversion with the Gregorian (AD) calendar, formatting,
and locale-aware output in Nepali (Devanagari) or English.

The library covers BS years 1970 to 2100 (approximately AD 1913 to 2043) with a
mathematically verified calendar data set. Every BS month's day count is hardcoded from
official Nepali government calendar records — not algorithmically approximated.

## Who built this?

Built by Kushal Khadka.
- GitHub: https://github.com/kushalkhadkaa
- LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/kushal-khadka-78a79996/
- Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/kushal_khadka/

## Core capabilities

- **Single, Range, and Multiple date selection** — three independent picker modes
- **BS to AD conversion** — bidirectional, accurate for 1970–2100 BS
- **22 built-in visual themes** — CSS Custom Property–based, trivially overridable
- **Bilingual output** — Nepali (NE) with Devanagari Unicode digits, or English (EN)
- **Inline time picker** — for single and range modes
- **9 range presets** — Today, This Week, This Month, Last Month, This Quarter, This Year, Last Year, Fiscal Year, Last Fiscal Year
- **Tithi (lunar day) tooltips** — traditional Nepali calendar annotation
- **Holiday markers** — configurable public holiday indicators
- **Fiscal year display** — Nepalese fiscal year (आ.व.) in header
- **Future-only / Past-only restriction** — min/max date constraints
- **Auto mask input** — typed date auto-formatted
- **WAI-ARIA accessibility** — keyboard navigation, screen reader labels
- **Mobile full-screen modal** — responsive layout for small screens
- **Offline ready** — no CDN, no internet, all assets local

## How to use it

```html
<!-- 1. Include the library -->
<link rel="stylesheet" href="dist/nepali-datepicker.css">
<script src="dist/nepali-datepicker.js"></script>

<!-- 2. Add an input -->
<input type="text" id="date" placeholder="YYYY-MM-DD">

<!-- 3. Initialize -->
<script>
  new NepaliDatePicker('#date', {
    theme: 'classic-light',
    lang: 'ne',
    format: 'YYYY-MM-DD'
  });
</script>
```

## Public API

```javascript
const picker = new NepaliDatePicker('#input', options);

picker.getDate()                          // returns selected date object { year, month, day }
picker.setDate({ year: 2082, month: 3, day: 15 })
picker.getRange()                         // returns { start, end } for range mode
picker.clear()                            // clears selection
picker.open()                             // opens calendar
picker.close()                            // closes calendar
picker.setTheme('glassmorphism')
picker.setLang('en')
picker.jumpTo(2082, 6)                    // navigate to year/month
picker.destroy()                          // removes instance and DOM
```

## Static helpers (always available globally)

```javascript
NepaliDatePicker.today()                  // today in BS { year, month, day }
NepaliDatePicker.bsToAd(2082, 3, 15)     // BS → AD Date object
NepaliDatePicker.adToBs(new Date())       // AD Date → BS { year, month, day }
NepaliDatePicker.version                  // '1.2.0'

AD2BS('2025-06-25')                       // '2082-03-11'
BS2AD('2082-03-11')                       // '2025-06-25'
```

## Configuration options

| Option          | Type      | Default         | Description                                      |
|-----------------|-----------|-----------------|--------------------------------------------------|
| theme           | string    | 'classic-light' | Theme key                                        |
| lang            | string    | 'ne'            | 'ne' (Nepali) or 'en' (English)                  |
| mode            | string    | 'single'        | 'single', 'range', or 'multiple'                 |
| format          | string    | 'YYYY-MM-DD'    | Output date format string                        |
| inline          | boolean   | false           | Render inline (not as popup)                     |
| minDate         | object    | null            | { year, month, day } minimum selectable date     |
| maxDate         | object    | null            | { year, month, day } maximum selectable date     |
| showAdDate      | boolean   | true            | Show AD date below each BS day cell              |
| showHolidays    | boolean   | false           | Show public holiday indicators                   |
| enableTime      | boolean   | false           | Enable inline time picker                        |
| presets         | boolean   | false           | Enable range presets sidebar                     |
| showFiscalYear  | boolean   | false           | Show Nepalese fiscal year in header              |
| futureOnly      | boolean   | false           | Disable past date selection                      |
| pastOnly        | boolean   | false           | Disable future date selection                    |
| unicodeDates    | boolean   | null            | Force Devanagari (true) or ASCII (false) digits  |
| weekStart       | number    | 0               | Week start day: 0 = Sunday, 1 = Monday           |

## Available themes (22)

classic-light, classic-dark, nepali-red, ocean-blue, forest-green, sunset-orange,
royal-purple, midnight, glassmorphism, neumorphism, gradient-aurora, minimal-mono,
pastel-soft, corporate-blue, earthy-terracotta, neon-cyberpunk, material-design,
retro-paper, high-contrast, festive-dashain, mountain-mist, tropical-teal

## Architecture

Three source modules compile to a single dist bundle:
- **ndp-data** — verified BS calendar data set (1970–2100) and holiday mappings
- **ndp-utils** — bidirectional BS/AD conversion engine and formatting helpers
- **ndp-core** — calendar UI, event system, public API, and theme switcher

Themes are implemented entirely via CSS Custom Properties scoped to wrapper classes.
No JavaScript is involved in styling. Custom themes require only CSS overrides.

## Keywords (for discoverability)

bs date picker, nepali calendar javascript library, bs to ad converter javascript,
nepali calendar widget, bikram sambat date picker, nepali js datepicker,
nepali date input, bs ad conversion library, nepali calendar library github,
free nepali datepicker, pure javascript nepali calendar, vanilla js nepali datepicker
